notes: CSA is the leading cloud security standards body. Industry standard (CCM used in thousands of organizations), vendor neutral, expanding into AI security. Publishes control frameworks (CCM, AICM), runs the STAR certification program (public compliance attestations), and conducts AI security research through its AI Safety Initiative.

data.description: The de facto standard for cloud security controls, organized by domains containing controls.
data.notes:
Version 4.0 released 2021 (197 controls), updated to v4.1 (207 controls, 283 CAIQ questions). 17 domains. Domain code + two-digit control number (e.g., IAM-01). Maps to ISO 27001, NIST, PCI-DSS. Used for STAR certification.

No per-control web pages exist. Resolution returns a download URL and extraction instructions. The download is a ZIP bundle containing multiple files: the main spreadsheet, implementation guidelines, auditing guidelines, mappings, and CAIQ questionnaire.

Extraction instructions: (1) Download the ZIP from the download URL. (2) Unzip the bundle. (3) Open the main Excel file (e.g., 'CCM+CAIQ v4.1.xlsx'). (4) Go to the 'CCM Controls' sheet/tab. (5) Columns: Control Domain (full name), Control Title, Control ID (format XXX-NN), Control Specification (full text). Additional columns contain applicability/ownership matrices, architectural relevance, and organizational relevance. (6) Find the row where Control ID matches.

Alternative: A machine-readable bundle (JSON/YAML/OSCAL) is available for programmatic access — preferred over parsing Excel.

Domain codes are 2-3 uppercase letters, except A&A (Audit & Assurance) which contains an ampersand. The pattern must account for this.

urls.note: Direct download (no account required). ZIP bundle containing Excel spreadsheet, CAIQ, implementation/auditing guidelines, and mappings.
Machine-readable bundle (JSON/YAML/OSCAL). Preferred for programmatic access over parsing Excel.

data.description: Security controls specific to AI/ML systems, complementing CCM for AI workloads.

versions_available.note: 243 controls across 18 domains. Incremental update from v1.0.

data.notes:
Version 1.0 released July 2025 (243 controls, updated to v1.0.3). Extends CCM for AI workloads. Shares all 17 CCM domains and adds MDS (Model Security) for 18 total. Control ID format is the same as CCM (XXX-NN), NOT the AI-prefixed codes previously assumed. Addresses model training security, data poisoning prevention, inference security, model theft protection, bias and fairness controls, and AI supply chain security. Aligns with NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, BSI AI C4.

Distinguishes between 'Cloud & AI Related' controls (shared with CCM) and 'Strictly AI Related' controls (AICM-only).

No per-control web pages exist. Resolution returns a download URL and extraction instructions. The download is a ZIP bundle containing: the main AICM spreadsheet, AI-CAIQ questionnaire, implementation guidelines, auditing guidelines, and framework mappings.

Extraction instructions: (1) Download the ZIP from the download URL. (2) Unzip the bundle. (3) Open the main AICM spreadsheet. (4) Go to the controls sheet. (5) Columns: Control Domain (full name), Control Title, Control ID (format XXX-NN), Specifications (full text). Additional columns for five pillars: Control Type, Control Applicability/Ownership, Architectural Relevance, LLM Lifecycle Relevance, Threat Category. (6) Find the row where Control ID matches.

Use both CCM and AICM for comprehensive AI cloud security coverage.

urls.note: Direct download (no account required). ZIP bundle containing AICM spreadsheet, AI-CAIQ, guidelines, and mappings.

data.description: Standardized self-assessment questionnaire mapping yes/no questions to CCM controls. One or more questions per CCM control.
data.notes:
CAIQ is the assessment-question form of CCM controls. Each CCM control has one or more yes/no questions used for cloud provider self-assessment and customer due diligence. CAIQ v4.0.3 contains 283 questions across the 207 CCM v4.1 controls.

Question IDs follow the format XXX-NN.M where XXX-NN is the parent CCM Control ID and M is the question sequence within that control (e.g., IAM-01.1, A&A-01.2). Multiple questions per control are common.

Distributed in the same ZIP bundle as CCM. The CAIQ is a separate sheet/tab within the bundle's main Excel file.

No per-question web pages exist. Resolution returns a download URL and extraction instructions.

Extraction instructions: (1) Download the CCM ZIP bundle. (2) Open the main Excel file (e.g., 'CCM+CAIQ v4.1.xlsx'). (3) Go to the 'CAIQ' sheet. (4) Columns: Question ID (XXX-NN.M format), Control ID (parent CCM control), Consensus Assessment Questions (the question text), Consensus Assessment Answers (where assessors record yes/no/NA). (5) Find the row where Question ID matches.

Used for STAR Level 1 self-assessments. Versions track CCM versions but with their own minor numbering.

urls.note: CAIQ ships in the same ZIP bundle as CCM v4.1.
Machine-readable bundle includes CAIQ in JSON/YAML/OSCAL formats.

match_nodes.description: AI Consensus Assessments Initiative Questionnaire (AICM) — CSA's branding is 'AI-CAIQ'

data.description: Standardized self-assessment questionnaire mapping yes/no questions to AICM controls. One or more questions per AICM control.
data.notes:
AI-CAIQ is the assessment-question form of AICM controls. Each AICM control has one or more yes/no questions used for AI cloud provider self-assessment.

Question IDs follow the format XXX-NN.M, identical to CAIQ format. Includes the MDS (Model Security) domain unique to AICM.

Distributed in the same ZIP bundle as AICM. The AI-CAIQ is a separate sheet within the AICM Excel file.

No per-question web pages exist. Resolution returns a download URL and extraction instructions.

Extraction: (1) Download the AICM ZIP bundle. (2) Open the AICM spreadsheet. (3) Go to the AI-CAIQ sheet. (4) Find the row where Question ID matches. The question text column contains the assessment question.

Used alongside CAIQ for comprehensive AI cloud security assessment.

urls.note: AI-CAIQ ships in the same ZIP bundle as AICM.

match_nodes.description: CSA STAR Registry — public registry of CAIQ submissions and third-party assessments. NOT a control framework.

data.official_name: Security, Trust, Assurance and Risk Registry
data.description: Public registry of cloud provider security assessments. The largest public collection of CAIQ submissions. Listed here for discoverability — STAR is an index/registry of assessments, not itself a set of controls. The underlying assessment instrument is CAIQ (see ccm-caiq).
data.notes:
STAR is a registry/index of submitted security assessments, not a control framework. It is included in the control namespace for discoverability — the controls being assessed are CCM (see secid:control/cloudsecurityalliance.org/ccm), and the assessment questionnaire is CAIQ (see secid:control/cloudsecurityalliance.org/ccm-caiq).

Three assessment levels:
- Level 1: Self-assessment via CAIQ (free; publicly downloadable submissions). The largest portion of the registry.
- Level 2: Third-party audit (STAR Certification based on ISO 27001, STAR Attestation based on SOC 2).
- Level 3: Continuous monitoring (planned/emerging).

The registry is publicly browsable and searchable at https://cloudsecurityalliance.org/star/registry. Each entry corresponds to a cloud provider organization and lists their submitted assessments; Level 1 CAIQ submissions are downloadable as Excel/PDF files.

No per-entry SecID pattern is currently defined — bare `star` resolves to the registry homepage. Future work could add a child pattern for organization slugs.

urls.note: Public searchable registry of CAIQ submissions and third-party attestations
Third-party search/analytics over the STAR registry
